sentence_reviews.text: An agent learns behavior from rewards while interacting with an environment over time.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto book page identifies Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction, 2nd ed.; AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning', including passive/active reinforcement learning, generalization, policy search, applications, and inverse reinforcement learning (lines 555-572).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected AIMA contents support RL topics, and the cited RL book is the canonical source, but the exact definition text was not directly visible in the inspected page.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Reinforcement learning is to connect Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory to downstream capabilities including Model-based reinforcement learning, Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 5 game theory and optimal decisions (lines 126-146), Chapter 17 'Making Complex Decisions' (lines 418-439), Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' including applications and inverse RL (lines 555-572), and Chapter 26.5 planning/control (lines 704-716).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports the listed areas as related AI topics and directly names inverse RL; the complete graph-role sentence is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572) and Chapter 22.6 'Applications of Reinforcement Learning' (line 568).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected AIMA contents support RL as the parent area; model-based RL is not directly named in those lines.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning' (line 569) under Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572).
sentence_reviews.notes: Inverse RL is directly named as an RL topic; imitation learning is not directly named in the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22.2 'Passive Reinforcement Learning' and 22.3 'Active Reinforcement Learning' (lines 557-565).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected AIMA contents support passive/active RL but do not directly name Bellman, temporal-difference, or value objectives in the visible lines.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 5 games (lines 126-146), Chapter 17 decisions (lines 418-439), Chapter 22 RL (lines 555-572), Chapter 26.5 planning/control (lines 704-716), and Chapter 27 AI limits/safety (lines 731-763).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports the related topic areas and broad limitations, but not the exact limitation wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Reinforcement learning uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 5 'Adversarial Search and Games' (lines 126-146), Chapter 17 'Making Complex Decisions' (lines 418-439), and Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572).
sentence_reviews.notes: Decision-theoretic context is supported; game theory is related but not core for all RL, so common strength is defensible.

sentence_reviews.text: Reinforcement learning uses Dynamical systems & control theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 1.2.7 'Control theory and cybernetics' (line 33), Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572), and Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control' (lines 704-716).
sentence_reviews.notes: Control context is supported, but the exact dynamical-systems dependency wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Reinforcement learning uses Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 17 'Making Complex Decisions' with sequential decision problems and MDPs (lines 418-439), Chapter 18 'Multiagent Decision Making' (lines 442-486), and Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA directly supports MDP/sequential-decision context and RL; the combined MDP/POMDP/belief-state label is broader than a direct quote.

sentence_reviews.text: Reinforcement learning uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572) and Chapter 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning' (line 569).
sentence_reviews.notes: Rewards are central to RL, but labels, targets, and preferences broaden the dependency beyond the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Reinforcement learning uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 17 'Making Complex Decisions' with sequential decision problems (lines 418-439) and Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' (lines 555-572).
sentence_reviews.notes: Sequential decision-making context supports the sequence/time dependency, though the exact atlas label is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Decision theory is supported as related to RL; game theory is common rather than core.
Control theory and planning/control links are supported, but the exact dependency is synthesized; common strength is defensible.
AIMA supports sequential decision problems and MDPs as context for RL; core strength is defensible under standard RL formalization.
Rewards are supported, but the broader supervision-signals label is not fully supported by inspected evidence.
RL is tied to sequential decisions/interactions over time; common strength is defensible.
